Let me think of the following algorithm:

But how to implement this in code?

The problem is that each time we modify the list, we need to start over and re-traverse the list from the beginning, using the prefix sum approach again.

This would be O(kn) time, where k is the number of passes. For example, in the worst case, each pass removes one node, leading to O(n^2) time. But for n=1000, this is 1e6 operations, which is acceptable.

So let's try to implement this approach.

The code would look something like this:

public ListNode removeZeroSumSublists(ListNode head) {
    ListNode dummy = new ListNode(0);
    dummy.next = head;
    while (true) {
        // Use prefix sum approach to find and remove one sequence
        HashMap<Integer, ListNode> prefixSum = new HashMap<>();
        int sum = 0;
        ListNode prev = dummy;
        ListNode current = dummy.next;
        boolean removed = false;
        while (current != null) {
            sum += current.val;
            if (prefixSum.containsKey(sum)) {
                // Remove nodes between prev.next and current
                ListNode start = prefixSum.get(sum).next;
                int s = sum + current.val; // Need to compute the sum of the nodes to remove?
                // Or just remove them and restart the process
                prefixSum.get(sum).next = current.next;
                // Reset the loop
                removed = true;
                break;
            } else {
                prefixSum.put(sum, prev);
            }
            prev = prev.next;
            current = current.next;
        }
        if (!removed) {
            break;
        }
    }
    return dummy.next;
}

public ListNode removeZeroSumSublists(ListNode head) {
    ListNode dummy = new ListNode(0);
    dummy.next = head;
    while (true) {
        // Use prefix sum approach to find and remove one sequence
        HashMap<Integer, ListNode> prefixSum = new HashMap<>();
        int sum = 0;
        ListNode prev = dummy;
        ListNode current = dummy.next;
        boolean removed = false;

            sum += current.val;
            if (prefixSum.containsKey(sum)) {
                // Remove nodes between prev.next and current

                prefixSum.get(sum).next = current.next;
                // Reset the loop
                removed = true;
                break;
            } else {
                prefixSum.put(sum, prev);
            }
            prev = prev.next;
            current = current.next;
        }
        if (!removed) {
            break;
        }
    }
    return dummy.next;
}
